name: Easy Pita Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, combine warm water and sugar; sprinkle in the dry yeast and let stand for 10 minutes or until dissolved and creamy.
2. Using an electric mixer, beat together 2 cups of the flour, vegetable oil, and salt. Beat for about 3 minutes or until smooth, scraping the side of the bowl occasionally.
3. With a wooden spoon, gradually beat in enough of the remaining flour to form a stiff dough. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for about 10 minutes or until smooth and elastic.
4. Place the dough in a lightly greased bowl, turning to grease all over. Cover with plastic wrap and let rise for 1 to 1 1/2 hours or until doubled in bulk.
5. Divide the dough into 16 or 32 pieces. On a lightly floured surface, roll each piece into rounds measuring about 7 inches or 4 inches.
6. Cover and let rise for 15 minutes or until slightly risen.
7. Meanwhile, preheat an ungreased baking sheet on the lowest rack of a 500°F (260°C) oven.
8. Using a floured metal spatula, quickly transfer 2 to 3 pita rounds onto the heated baking sheet. Bake for 3 to 4 minutes or until puffed and light golden around the edges.
9. Repeat with the remaining pita rounds. Let cool between damp tea towels. Pitas will collapse slightly but retain a pocket.
10. Alternatively, if crisp pitas are desired, let cool on racks. Pitas can be stored in plastic bags in the freezer for up to 1 month. Makes 16 7-inch pitas or 32 4-inch pitas.
